summary: Rework the BI dashboard into an overview cockpit and a three-perspective analysis workbench.

notes: No schema changes in this release.
The dashboard analysis workbench continues to use the existing psa_dashboard_analysis contract.
Project, employee, and department analysis views are derived by frontend aggregation from the existing response payload.
Repositioned the dashboard as a two-level experience: overview cockpit for quick status and analysis workbench for drill-down.
Added project, employee, and department perspectives to the analysis workbench.
Added clickable overview insight cards that jump directly into the relevant analysis perspective.
Kept previous dashboard data visible during refresh and uses skeleton loading only for the initial empty state.
Stabilized period controls as concrete period selectors followed by year/quarter/month/week grain switching.
Improved dark-mode chart readability for trend area charts and chart axes.
Shortened analysis sort labels to avoid wrapping and adjusted the analysis grid to give the central charts more room.

verification: Run npm --prefix frontend run build with VITE_APP_VERSION=V0.18.22 and production VITE variables.
Run deploy/scripts/pre-deploy-check.sh on the cloud host with EXPECTED_VERSION=V0.18.22.
Run scripts/smoke-frontend-render.mjs against production with EXPECTED_VERSION=V0.18.22.
known_warnings: Vite may still warn that some chunks are larger than 500 kB.
The new employee and department analysis perspectives are frontend aggregations; backend aggregation can be added later if production data volume requires it.
